revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Forbidden colors (Japan)]][[ja:禁色]][[ru:Запрещённый цвет]][[tr:Kinjiki]][[uk:Заборонений колір]][[zh:禁色 (衣服令)]]
{{Short description|Colors used by aristocracy in Heian-kyō}} {{more footnotes|date=April 2022}} {| class="standard" style="float:right" ! colspan="2" | Forbidden сolors |- ! [[:en:RGB|RGB]] ! Name |- | style="background-color:#dd9159; padding:10px;" | #DD9159 | {{ill|Sumac|en|Sumac|qid=Q157649|lt=Sumac|1=Sumac}}-dyed |- | style="background-color:#6C9956; padding:10px;; color:#ffffff;" | #6C9956 | Pale oak |- | style="background-color:#E06351; padding:10px;" | #E06351 | Sawtooth oak |- | style="background-color:#FC7F31; padding:10px;" | #FC7F31 | {{ill|Ochre|en|Ochre|qid=Q194191|lt=Ochre|1=ochre}} |- | style="background-color:#561649; padding:10px; color:#ffffff;" | #561649 | Deep purple |- | style="background-color:#FBB92D; padding:10px;" | #FBB92D | Rich {{ill|gardenia|en|gardenia|qid=Q740887|lt=gardenia|1=Gardenia}} |- | style="background-color:#A40522; padding:10px; color:#ffffff;" | #A40522 | Deep scarlet |- | style="background-color:#790505; padding:10px; color:#ffffff;" | #790505 | Sappanwood |}
The system of {{nihongo|'''forbidden colors'''|禁色|kinjiki}} developed in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} in the 8th century saw {{ill|traditional colors of Japan|en|traditional colors of Japan|qid=Q2607820|lt=certain colors|1=Traditional colors of Japan}} of [[:en:Traditional Japanese clothing|traditional court clothing]] reserved for certain Ranks of court official at the {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|qid=Q751907|lt=Imperial Court|1=Heian-kyō}} in {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. The hierarchy of colors was developed under the {{transl|ja|[[Ritsuryō]]}} system.
==History== {{nihongo|{{ill|Sumac|en|Sumac|qid=Q157649|lt=Sumac|1=Sumac}} dye|黄櫨染|kōrozen}} was used only for the outerwear of the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}. It was banned for use by anyone except the Japanese monarch. To this day, the ceremonial clothes of the Emperor worn during the enthronement ceremony are dyed in this color, extracted from sumac (''{{ill|Toxicodendron succedaneum|en|Toxicodendron succedaneum|qid=Q130143|lt=Toxicodendron succedaneum|1=Toxicodendron succedaneum}}''), a Japanese wax tree.
Seven additional colors were installed in the 10th and 11th centuries: * {{nihongo|Pale oak|青白橡|aoshirotsurubami}} is the color of the outerrobes of the Emperor of Japan. As an exception, this color could be worn by the concubines and mistresses of the monarch, who were in the position of palace servants of the 4th Rank, as well as secretaries of the treasury of the 6th Rank. * {{nihongo|Sawtooth oak|赤白橡|akashirotsurubami}} is the color of the outerwear of the {{ill|Daijō Tennō|en|Daijō Tennō|qid=Q14303973|lt=ex-Emperor of Japan|1=Daijō Tennō}}. * {{nihongo|{{ill|Ochre|en|Ochre|qid=Q194191|lt=Ochre|1=ochre}}|黄丹|1=Ōdan|ōtan}} is the color of the outerwear of the crown prince of Japan. * {{nihongo|Deep purple|深紫|kokimurasaki}} is the outerwear color of 1st Rank aristocrats. * {{nihongo|Rich {{ill|gardenia|en|gardenia|qid=Q740887|lt=gardenia|1=Gardenia}}|支子|kokikuchinashi}} is a reserve forbidden color, a substitute for ochre. Produced from the fruits of gardenia (''{{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|en|Gardenia jasminoides|qid=Q1072101|lt=Gardenia jasminoides|1=Gardenia jasminoides}}''), permission to wear this color could be granted by imperial rescript. * {{nihongo|Deep scarlet|深緋|kokiake}} is an alternate forbidden color, a substitute for deep purple. Permission to wear this color could be granted by imperial rescript. * {{nihongo|Sappanwood|蘇芳|su'ō}} is a reserve forbidden color, a substitute for deep purple. Produced from the fruit of ''{{ill|Biancaea sappan|en|Biancaea sappan|qid=Q56088062|lt=Biancaea sappan|1=Biancaea sappan}}'', permission to wear this color could be granted by imperial rescript.
During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}} (1867–1911) of the 19th century, the ban was lifted from all colors except sumac, ochre and gardenia.
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|author=Nihon Shikisai Gakkai|title=Shinpen shikisai kagaku handobukku|publisher=Tokyo Daigaku Shuppankai|date=1985|isbn=4-13-061000-7|language=ja}} * {{cite book|last = Shaver |first =R. |title = Kabuki Costume |url = https://books.google.com/books?id=Pf3PAgAAQBAJ |publisher= Tuttle Publishing |date = 2013 |pages = 79 |isbn = 9781462903986}}
[[en:Forbidden colors (Japan)]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:禁色]] [[ru:Запрещённый цвет]] [[tr:Kinjiki]] [[uk:Заборонений колір]] [[zh:禁色 (衣服令)]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11591157}} [[Category:Cultural history of Japan]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 22 May 2025]] [[Category:Special:LonelyPages 26 May 2025]] [[Category:Textile arts of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
